title: Denzel Bentley Elevated to WBO Middleweight Champion
dek: Britain's Denzel Bentley was elevated to full WBO middleweight champion after Kazakhstan boxer Janibek Alimkhanuly relinquished the title, according to BBC Sport. Alimkhanuly tested positive for a banned substance in December and was suspended for one year.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Bentley inherits the WBO middleweight title through relinquishment, not in-ring competition, after Alimkhanuly's suspension and his stated intention to move up to super middleweight.
bottom_line: Bentley now holds the WBO middleweight title, but the path to it was administrative, not competitive. Watch whether he pursues an active defense against a ranked challenger, and whether Alimkhanuly's intended move to super middleweight materializes after his suspension ends.
body: Britain's Denzel Bentley was elevated to full WBO middleweight champion after Kazakhstan boxer Janibek Alimkhanuly relinquished the belt, according to BBC Sport. The elevation represents a title change by administrative procedure rather than in-ring competition.

Alimkhanuly tested positive for a banned substance in December and was subsequently suspended for one year, per BBC Sport. The suspension and his stated intention to move up to super middleweight prompted him to cede the middleweight title he held. Bentley's ascension to the vacant championship followed that relinquishment.
Bentley's recent resume includes a victory over Endry Saavedra as part of the Dereck Chisora versus Deontay Wilder card in April, according to BBC Sport.

The relinquishment marks a title change in professional boxing driven by circumstances outside the ring: a suspension and a fighter's decision to chase opportunities at a heavier weight class. Bentley becomes the WBO middleweight champion without having defeated the incumbent in sanctioned competition.
